title: mitella koshiensis var. furusei
intro: Mitella koshiensis var. furusei, also known as the Furuse's mitella or Japanese mitella, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the Saxifragaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to Japan, known for its unique, lobed leaves and white, bell-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and reduce pain. Medicinal preparations of Mitella koshiensis var. furusei include infusions and decoctions made from the roots and leaves, which are said to promote overall health and well-being by reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health.
